name: BILL Spend & Expense (Divvy) Corporate Card API
what_it_does: BILL Spend & Expense (formerly Divvy) REST API for corporate card and expense management platform. Enables AI agents to manage corporate card issuance and limit controls, handle budget creation and spend tracking by team/department, access transaction data and expense categorization, retrieve receipt capture and document management, manage approval workflow and reimbursement, handle virtual card creation and vendor-specific cards, access real-time spend analytics and reporting, retrieve employee expense submission and review data, manage merchant controls and category restrictions, and integrate spend data with QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, and BILL AP.
use_cases: Managing corporate card controls and budget limits from spend management agents
Accessing transaction and expense data from financial operations agents
Handling receipt capture and expense categorization from accounting automation agents
Integrating spend data with accounting from financial automation agents
not_for: Large enterprise with Concur or Coupa enterprise T&E management
International expense management without US-first SMB focus
Complex multi-currency travel expense without enterprise T&E platform
best_when: A small-to-mid-size business using BILL Spend & Expense (Divvy) wants AI agents to automate corporate card management, budget tracking, expense categorization, receipt processing, and accounting integration.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate card limit automation must prevent fraud and unauthorized spending. Virtual card creation and limit changes affect real financial accounts. Budget depletion alerts are critical — automated card issuance without budget checks overspends approved amounts. Employee spend data is sensitive PII.

auth.notes: BILL Spend & Expense uses OAuth 2.0 (inherited from BILL platform API). Account and member-level credentials with spend, transactions, and budgets scopes. Integrated with BILL developer portal. Webhooks for transaction and budget events. Part of BILL integrated platform with AP automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited corporate cards, no software fees; revenue from interchange
pricing.paid_tiers: Free base platform; optional premium features; interchange-funded model
pricing.notes: San Jose, California. BILL subsidiary (acquired Divvy 2021 for $2.5B). Founded 2016 (as Divvy). Corporate card and expense management for SMB. Interchange-revenue business model makes base product free. 10,000+ customers. Integrated with BILL AP platform. Competes with Ramp and Brex for SMB corporate card and expense management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; transaction query throttled

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Card limit automation must check budget availability — automated increase without budget check overspends
Virtual card creation for vendors should not be automated without approval — fraud risk for unauthorized vendors
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API via BILL developer portal
Divvy brand may still appear in API endpoints post-BILL integration — use BILL platform documentation
Employee transaction data is sensitive PII — restrict API access to finance team use cases only
Merchant category restrictions — automated changes to card controls affect employee ability to complete purchases
agent_readiness.security_notes: Corporate card and expense data. SOC2, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US data residency. Employee spend data and card control sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate card and expense data. SOC2, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US data residency. Employee spend data and fraud controls.
